refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation in the problem domain; recording and managing are implementation concerns that belong in the DP/IM layers. The refined FR focuses on the core functional need: translating mission context into measurable performance targets. This remains solution-neutral and avoids prescribing how derivation, storage, or lifecycle management are realized.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that given a worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production targets, safety constraints), the system produces a documented set of performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that are traceable to input parameters and accepted by the Site Manager and Safety Manager.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall accept worksite mission context inputs (e.g., site layout, production targets, safety constraints, operational policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall compute performance requirements (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements in a retrievable format.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The NGHE solution shall enable traceability between worksite inputs and derived performance requirements.
refined_fr.note: The original FR is not atomic; it has been decomposed into five atomic sub-FRs. CR-NGHE-005 itself now serves as a parent functional requirement. Each sub-FR addresses a single obligation and remains solution-neutral.

candidate_dps: DP-NGHE-005.1: Establish a parameterized mission-context capture mechanism that ingests worksite operational inputs (safety plan constraints, mission objectives, environmental conditions) and maps them to a standardized performance requirement template, with version control and traceability metadata.

DP-NGHE-005.2: Implement a rule-based inference engine that translates worksite mission parameters (hazard classification, operational tempo, personnel density, equipment constraints) into quantified performance targets (response time, accuracy, coverage) via a documented decision matrix.

DP-NGHE-005.3: Design a baseline registry and change-control process that stores performance target snapshots, records calibration/update events with timestamps and justification, and maintains a queryable audit trail linking each target to its originating worksite context.

DP-NGHE-005.4: Provide a human-in-the-loop validation workflow where domain experts (safety officers, mission planners) review derived performance targets against worksite policies before acceptance, with sign-off and exception logging.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-NGHE-005.1 + DP-NGHE-005.3 + DP-NGHE-005.4 (integrated): Establish a parameterized mission-context capture and standardized requirement template system (DP-NGHE-005.1) coupled with a persistent baseline registry and change-control audit trail (DP-NGHE-005.3), and enforce human-in-the-loop validation and sign-off (DP-NGHE-005.4) to ensure performance targets are derived from worksite inputs, documented, traceable, and maintained with full historical provenance.

potential_sources: DP-NGHE-005.1 and DP-NGHE-005.3 share a common data model (performance target schema and metadata); this is acceptable coupling because both operate on the same logical artifact (the requirement baseline) and separation would introduce duplication.

DP-NGHE-005.4 (validation workflow) depends on outputs of DP-NGHE-005.1 (derived targets) and feeds into DP-NGHE-005.3 (registry); this is a sequential dependency, not a circular coupling, and is architecturally sound.

DP-NGHE-005.2 (rule-based inference) is not selected in the primary solution; if included, it would introduce coupling to domain-specific decision logic that may vary by worksite type and could complicate maintenance. Recommend deferring to a secondary refinement or treating as an optional enhancement.
coupling_check.notes: The selected combination (DP-NGHE-005.1 + DP-NGHE-005.3 + DP-NGHE-005.4) exhibits low coupling because each DP addresses a distinct concern: capture (input), persistence (storage/audit), and validation (control gate). The data flow is unidirectional (capture → validation → registry), avoiding feedback loops. Interfaces are well-defined: DP-NGHE-005.1 outputs a candidate requirement set; DP-NGHE-005.4 consumes it and produces a validated set; DP-NGHE-005.3 stores and retrieves both. This supports independent evolution and testing.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is expressible in a finite, standardized parameter set (e.g., hazard class, operational tempo, personnel count). RISK: If worksites exhibit highly heterogeneous or emergent mission profiles, the template may be too rigid. Mitigation: Design the template with extensible fields and periodic review cycles.

ASSUMPTION: Domain experts (safety officers, mission planners) are available and willing to participate in validation workflows. RISK: If validation becomes a bottleneck or is bypassed, traceability and acceptance criteria are compromised. Mitigation: Define clear SLAs for validation review and escalation paths for exceptions.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plans and operational policies are documented and accessible in machine-readable or structured form. RISK: If policies are informal, oral, or inconsistently documented, the capture mechanism may miss critical constraints. Mitigation: Establish a worksite documentation standard and audit process.

ASSUMPTION: The baseline registry and audit trail can be maintained with acceptable performance and storage overhead. RISK: If the system accumulates large volumes of historical data, query and retrieval latency may degrade. Mitigation: Implement archival and indexing strategies; define retention policies.

ASSUMPTION: The FR is satisfied by deriving targets from worksite context alone; no external benchmarking or industry standards are required. RISK: If regulatory or industry standards impose additional constraints, the derived targets may be insufficient. Mitigation: Extend DP-NGHE-005.1 to include a standards-mapping input and DP-NGHE-005.4 to validate against external requirements.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR-NGHE-005 is well-formed and the selected DP set is sound. Recommend the following refinements: (1) Explicitly define the worksite mission-context parameter schema (e.g., hazard classification taxonomy, operational tempo ranges, personnel density thresholds) as a separate design artifact (DP-NGHE-005.1a) to support reuse and standardization. (2) Clarify the scope of 'performance targets': are these quantitative metrics (e.g., detection latency ≤ 2 seconds) or qualitative objectives (e.g., 'high reliability')? Recommend quantitative targets to support traceability and acceptance testing. (3) Define the validation workflow in DP-NGHE-005.4 with explicit roles, approval criteria, and exception handling to avoid ambiguity. (4) Consider adding a secondary DP-NGHE-005.5 to address periodic re-derivation or re-validation of targets (e.g., when worksite mission changes), ensuring the baseline remains current. (5) Introduce an IM (Implementation Module) layer to specify whether the capture mechanism, registry, and validation workflow are realized as a single integrated tool, separate services, or manual processes; this will inform downstream architectural decisions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized mission-context capture, requirement templating, baseline registry, change-control audit trail, and human-in-the-loop validation orchestration for performance target derivation and provenance tracking.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context, worksite inputs, and performance target specifications from external tasking authority; feeds parameterized mission-context capture system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exports baseline registry state, change-control audit trail, and validation sign-off records for external monitoring and compliance verification.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety-critical constraint updates that trigger requirement re-validation; exports updated safety-derived performance targets back to Safety Services for enforcement.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest cohesion: mission-context capture, requirement templating, baseline registry, change-control audit, and validation orchestration are all governance and metadata-management functions that logically belong in the solution-level control plane. Minimal diffusion: only exports audit records and receives tasking inputs via well-defined interfaces. Human-in-the-loop validation can be implemented as a service within this boundary.

Poor fit: Vehicle is a physical/operational asset, not a governance or metadata layer. Requirement templating, baseline registry, and audit trails are solution-wide concerns that would be scattered across vehicle firmware and control logic. High diffusion: would require Vehicle to maintain global state and coordinate validation across multiple instances.

Poor fit: Teleoperation is an operational mode/interface, not a governance layer. Mission-context capture and baseline registry are solution-level concerns, not teleoperation-specific. High diffusion: requirement templates and audit trails would need to be replicated or synchronized across teleoperation and autonomous modes.

Partial fit: Safety Services would naturally own safety-derived performance targets and their validation, but the broader mission-context capture, requirement templating, and baseline registry are cross-cutting governance concerns that extend beyond safety. Moderate-to-high diffusion: mission-level requirements and audit trails would need to diffuse to other solution components.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The integrated DP (mission-context capture + baseline registry + change-control audit + human-in-the-loop validation) is fundamentally a solution-level governance and metadata-management function. It establishes the authoritative source of truth for performance targets, their derivation, and their historical provenance. This is a cross-cutting concern that must be centralized to avoid duplication, inconsistency, and loss of audit trail integrity. Containment within NGHE Solution ensures single ownership, unified state management, and clear external interfaces for tasking input and compliance export.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic governance layer, it may accumulate unrelated responsibilities over time. Mitigation: Define clear service boundaries within NGHE Solution (e.g., Mission Context Service, Baseline Registry Service, Audit Service, Validation Orchestrator) to maintain internal cohesion.

Risk: Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may need to query or subscribe to baseline registry and audit trail updates. Mitigation: Establish well-defined read-only interfaces (e.g., via Telemetry or a dedicated Registry Query interface) to allow downstream consumers to access baseline state without coupling them to the governance layer's internal implementation.

Risk: Human-in-the-loop validation may introduce latency or bottlenecks if not properly designed. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous validation workflows with clear escalation paths and timeout policies to prevent mission delays.

Risk: Change-control audit trail may grow unbounded, impacting performance and storage. Mitigation: Implement retention policies, archival strategies, and efficient indexing to maintain audit trail integrity without degrading system performance.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision to DP choice is recommended. The integrated DP is well-suited to NGHE Solution as the governance and mission-control plane. However, to improve clarity and reduce future diffusion risk, consider decomposing the DP into three tightly-coupled sub-DPs: (1) Mission-Context Capture and Requirement Templating (DP-NGHE-005.1), (2) Persistent Baseline Registry and Change-Control Audit (DP-NGHE-005.3), and (3) Human-in-the-Loop Validation Orchestration (DP-NGHE-005.4). Each sub-DP should map to a distinct service or module within NGHE Solution, with clear internal interfaces and ownership. This maintains cohesion at the solution level while enabling independent testing, versioning, and evolution of each governance function.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-NGHE-005.1 + DP-NGHE-005.3 + DP-NGHE-005.4 (integrated) directly and sufficiently addresses FR-NGHE-005. The three sub-DPs form a coherent solution: parameterized mission-context capture (DP-005.1) establishes the input mechanism; persistent baseline registry with change-control audit (DP-005.3) ensures traceability and historical provenance; human-in-the-loop validation (DP-005.4) enforces governance. Together, they satisfy the requirement to 'derive performance requirements from worksite mission context' with full documentation and traceability. Mapping is logically sound and complete.

diffusion_risks: MODERATE: If baseline registry and audit trail queries are not exposed via well-defined read-only interfaces, downstream modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) may attempt to replicate or cache baseline state locally, creating inconsistency and diffusion. Mitigation: Establish a Registry Query Service interface within NGHE Solution with clear SLAs for read access.

MODERATE: Human-in-the-loop validation orchestration may diffuse if validation logic is embedded in multiple operational modes (autonomous vs. teleoperation). Mitigation: Centralize validation orchestrator as a single service within NGHE Solution; operational modes invoke it via a standardized validation request/response interface.

LOW-TO-MODERATE: Change-control audit trail may accumulate unbounded state, creating performance and storage risks if retention/archival policies are not defined upfront. Mitigation: Define and enforce retention policies, archival strategies, and efficient indexing as part of IM design.

LOW: Mission-context capture may receive inputs from multiple external sources (Tasking, Safety Services) with potential for conflicting or incomplete data. Mitigation: Establish clear input validation and conflict-resolution rules within the mission-context capture service.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment score of 0.92 for NGHE Solution is justified. The integrated DP is fundamentally a solution-level governance and metadata-management function that must be centralized to maintain single source of truth, avoid duplication, and preserve audit trail integrity. All three sub-DPs (mission-context capture, baseline registry, change-control audit, validation orchestration) are cross-cutting concerns that logically belong in the solution-level control plane. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) are well-defined and minimize coupling. Recommended internal decomposition into three tightly-coupled services (Mission Context Service, Baseline Registry Service, Audit Service, Validation Orchestrator) will maintain cohesion while enabling independent testing and evolution. No alternative IM boundary (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) offers better containment without introducing high diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. Acceptance criteria are directly verifiable: (1) 'System produces a documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' can be verified by querying the Baseline Registry Service and validating that each performance target has a documented derivation path from Tasking inputs. (2) 'Maintains a history of baselines/calibration updates' can be verified by auditing the Change-Control Audit Trail Service for completeness and integrity of historical records. (3) Compliance with 'Worksite safety plan and operational policies' can be verified by examining validation sign-off records and ensuring that all performance targets have passed human-in-the-loop validation before baseline commitment. All verification activities can be automated via compliance queries against the NGHE Solution's governance layer.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: HIGH. Allocation is clear and unambiguous: NGHE Solution owns the entire FR-NGHE-005 realization. Ownership boundaries are explicit: Mission Context Service owns parameterized capture and templating; Baseline Registry Service owns persistent storage and state management; Audit Service owns change-control and historical provenance; Validation Orchestrator owns human-in-the-loop sign-off. External interfaces are well-defined: Tasking provides inputs, Telemetry exports audit records and baseline state, SafetyAlert triggers re-validation. No ambiguity regarding which module is responsible for which aspect of the DP. Allocation can be directly mapped to development teams and testing strategies.

recommended_revisions: DECOMPOSE DP into three explicit sub-DPs with distinct service ownership within NGHE Solution: (1) DP-NGHE-005.1-SVC: Mission-Context Capture and Requirement Templating Service; (2) DP-NGHE-005.3-SVC: Persistent Baseline Registry and Change-Control Audit Service; (3) DP-NGHE-005.4-SVC: Human-in-the-Loop Validation Orchestration Service. This maintains solution-level cohesion while enabling independent testing, versioning, and evolution.

DEFINE Registry Query Interface: Establish a read-only interface (e.g., 'RegistryQuery') within NGHE Solution to allow Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to query baseline state and audit trail without coupling to internal implementation. Include SLAs for query latency and availability.

ESTABLISH Validation Request/Response Protocol: Define a standardized interface for operational modes (autonomous, teleoperation) to invoke human-in-the-loop validation. Include timeout policies, escalation paths, and fallback behaviors to prevent mission delays.

DEFINE Retention and Archival Policies: Specify retention periods for audit trail records, archival strategies (e.g., cold storage), and indexing schemes to manage unbounded growth while preserving audit integrity.

CLARIFY Input Conflict Resolution: Document rules for handling conflicting or incomplete mission-context inputs from multiple sources (Tasking, Safety Services). Define precedence, validation thresholds, and escalation triggers.

ADD Traceability Metadata: Ensure that each performance target in the Baseline Registry includes explicit traceability metadata: source worksite input, derivation timestamp, validation sign-off record, and change history. This directly supports acceptance criteria verification.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence, confirming that FR-NGHE-005 is sufficiently addressed by the integrated DP and can be realized within NGHE Solution with acceptable containment (0.92). However, the recommended_revisions from the DP-IM gate reveal that the current DP formulation, while logically sound, lacks explicit architectural granularity needed to guide implementation and mitigate diffusion risks in a safety-critical system. Specifically: (1) The three sub-DPs (mission-context capture, baseline registry, validation orchestration) are currently implicit within the integrated DP statement; making them explicit as service-level design parameters will improve traceability, testability, and ownership clarity. (2) Four critical interface and policy definitions (Registry Query Interface, Validation Request/Response Protocol, Retention/Archival Policies, Input Conflict Resolution) are identified as necessary to prevent downstream diffusion and ensure safety compliance. (3) Given high safety criticality, explicit architectural decomposition at the DP level is justified to enable rigorous verification and reduce implementation ambiguity. Proceeding directly to STOP would defer these critical architectural decisions to implementation, increasing risk of rework and safety gaps. ZIGZAG_DOWN (deriving sub-FRs) is premature because the current FR is already well-scoped and the gates confirm it does not require functional decomposition; rather, it requires DP-level architectural refinement. REVISE_CURRENT_LEVEL is the appropriate path: refine the DP formulation to explicitly name the three service-level sub-DPs and their interfaces, then re-validate against the gates before proceeding to implementation or further decomposition.

revise.why: The current DP formulation (DP-NGHE-005.1 + DP-NGHE-005.3 + DP-NGHE-005.4 integrated) is logically correct but architecturally implicit. To satisfy the DP-IM gate's recommended_revisions and reduce implementation risk in a safety-critical context, the DP must be explicitly decomposed into three service-level design parameters with clear ownership and interface contracts: (1) DP-NGHE-005.1-SVC: Mission-Context Capture and Requirement Templating Service—owns parameterized input capture, templating, and conflict resolution from Tasking and Safety Services. (2) DP-NGHE-005.3-SVC: Persistent Baseline Registry and Change-Control Audit Service—owns persistent storage of performance baselines, change-control audit trail, retention/archival policies, and read-only query interface for downstream consumers. (3) DP-NGHE-005.4-SVC: Human-in-the-Loop Validation Orchestration Service—owns validation request/response protocol, sign-off orchestration, timeout/escalation policies, and integration with operational modes (autonomous, teleoperation). Each sub-DP must include explicit interface definitions (Registry Query Interface, Validation Request/Response Protocol) and policy specifications (Retention/Archival, Input Conflict Resolution, Traceability Metadata) to prevent diffusion and ensure safety compliance. This refinement maintains solution-level cohesion while enabling independent testing, versioning, and allocation to development teams.

assumptions: FR-NGHE-005 scope is correctly bounded to performance-requirement derivation, baseline management, and validation governance; no functional decomposition is required.
NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for all three sub-DPs; no alternative packaging (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) offers better containment without introducing high diffusion.
Downstream modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) will consume baseline state and audit records via well-defined read-only interfaces; no local replication or caching is permitted.
Human-in-the-loop validation is a synchronous, blocking operation that must complete before baseline commitment; timeout and escalation policies are defined to prevent mission delays.
Mission-context inputs from Tasking and Safety Services may conflict or be incomplete; conflict-resolution rules and validation thresholds are defined upfront.
Audit trail retention and archival policies are feasible within operational constraints (storage, query latency, compliance requirements).
Traceability metadata (source, timestamp, validation sign-off, change history) can be captured and queried efficiently for verification and compliance audits.
open_risks: MODERATE: If Registry Query Interface SLAs are not met (e.g., query latency exceeds operational thresholds), downstream modules may implement local caching or replication, creating consistency risks. Mitigation: Define and validate SLAs during DP refinement; include performance testing in acceptance criteria.

MODERATE: Validation orchestration may become a bottleneck if human-in-the-loop sign-off is required for every baseline update in high-frequency operational scenarios. Mitigation: Define escalation paths and automated validation rules for low-risk updates; reserve human sign-off for high-impact changes.

MODERATE: Audit trail may accumulate unbounded state if retention/archival policies are not enforced. Mitigation: Define retention periods, archival triggers, and indexing schemes during IM design; include storage monitoring and alerting.

LOW-TO-MODERATE: Input conflict resolution rules may be ambiguous or incomplete, leading to inconsistent baseline derivation. Mitigation: Formalize conflict-resolution logic as part of DP refinement; include test cases for common conflict scenarios.

LOW: Traceability metadata may be incomplete or inconsistent if capture is not enforced at all baseline-update touchpoints. Mitigation: Implement metadata validation and audit checks within Mission Context Service and Baseline Registry Service.
evidence_needed: Refined DP formulation with explicit service-level sub-DPs (DP-NGHE-005.1-SVC, DP-NGHE-005.3-SVC, DP-NGHE-005.4-SVC) and interface contracts (Registry Query Interface, Validation Request/Response Protocol).
Formal specification of Retention and Archival Policies: retention periods, archival triggers, cold-storage strategy, indexing scheme, and performance/storage impact analysis.
Formal specification of Input Conflict Resolution Rules: precedence logic, validation thresholds, escalation triggers, and test cases for common conflict scenarios.
Formal specification of Traceability Metadata Schema: required fields (source, timestamp, validation sign-off, change history), capture points, validation rules, and query interface.
SLA definitions for Registry Query Interface: latency targets, availability targets, throughput capacity, and performance testing plan.
Timeout and Escalation Policies for Validation Orchestration: timeout thresholds, escalation paths, fallback behaviors, and impact on mission execution.
Re-validation of DP-IM gate after DP refinement to confirm that explicit service-level decomposition and interface definitions reduce diffusion risks to acceptable levels.
Safety-criticality assessment: confirmation that traceability, audit trail integrity, and validation governance meet safety requirements (e.g., DO-178C, IEC 61508) for the target operational context.
